topic.what: Evaluation of Shaoyao Gancao Decoction (SGD) — a two-herb formula combining Paeonia lactiflora (white peony root) and Glycyrrhiza uralensis (licorice) — for chronic atrophic gastritis, assessing antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties in an animal model.
topic.how: In vivo rodent model of chronic atrophic gastritis; SGD administered orally at multiple dose levels; endpoints include gastric smooth muscle contractility, histopathological atrophy and metaplasia scores, mast cell counts, inflammatory cytokine levels (IL-6, IL-1β, COX-2), and oxidative stress markers.
topic.why_matters: SGD is one of the oldest documented two-herb formulas in TCM, historically used for abdominal spasm and pain; characterising its gastroprotective activity in atrophic gastritis provides mechanistic grounding for clinical use and illustrates the power of minimal-herb formulae for targeted pharmacological action.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction in gastric smooth muscle hypercontractility in gastritis model
characteristics.value: Significant reduction in contractility indices at mid and high SGD doses
characteristics.evidence: Ex vivo smooth muscle contractility assay on isolated gastric strips
characteristics.context: Antispasmodic action attributed to paeoniflorin (Paeonia) and glycyrrhizin/isoliquiritigenin (Glycyrrhiza) synergy

characteristics.what_it_measures: Reduction of inflammatory mediators in gastric mucosa
characteristics.value: Decreased IL-6, IL-1β, and COX-2 expression in treated vs. model groups
characteristics.evidence: ELISA and immunohistochemistry on gastric tissue
characteristics.context: Anti-inflammatory effects of licorice glycyrrhizin are well-established; peony paeoniflorin contributes via T-cell regulation

characteristics.value: Partial improvement in gland density and mucosal thickness at therapeutic doses
characteristics.evidence: H&E staining scored by blinded pathologist
characteristics.context: Structural repair partial, suggesting adjunctive rather than standalone therapy for established atrophy

good_for: Researchers studying minimal TCM formulas for targeted pharmacological dissection
Patients with atrophic gastritis and predominant spasm/pain presentation
KB articles on paeoniflorin and glycyrrhizin pharmacology in gastric disease
not_for: H. pylori eradication as primary goal
Advanced intestinal metaplasia without additional formula components
requires: Dose calibration — glycyrrhizin has known mineralocorticoid side-effects at high dose
Confirmation in human clinical studies before prescribing

novelty: Demonstrates that a two-herb classical formula achieves multi-pathway effect (antispasmodic + anti-inflammatory + partial mucosal repair) in a single animal study
Provides mechanistic rationale for the classical clinical indication of SGD in gastric pain and cramp
open_problems: Optimal peony:licorice ratio for atrophic gastritis not optimised (classical 1:1 assumed, not tested)
Long-term safety of glycyrrhizin in chronic administration not assessed in this model
Human RCT evidence for SGD in atrophic gastritis specifically is lacking
